frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Look, I understand women have genuine safety concerns, but this habit of treating every man like a potential threat is neither fair nor practical. Most of us are decent, professional people—I've never behaved inappropriately with any woman colleague—so why should normal men keep getting judged for the actions of a few? Frankly, men also face risks in this country, but nobody seems interested in hearing that side.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it recenters male feelings in response to women's safety concerns and uses a classic 'not all men' dismissal. It encodes generalization policing, self-exemption ('I've never behaved inappropriately'), and deflection to men's safety.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
